channel.title: FuelWired — Oil & Gas Intelligence
channel.description: Real-time oil and gas industry news, energy market analysis, and LNG market intelligence.

item.title: EIA Sees USA Energy Demand Dropping in 2026, Rising in 2027
item.description: In its latest short term energy outlook, the U.S. Energy Information Administration projected that U.S. total energy consumption will decrease in 2026 then rise in 2027.

item.title: Traffic Flows Through Hormuz Despite Ship Attack
item.description: Traffic continued to flow in both directions through the vital thoroughfare on Friday.

item.title: USA Oil, Gas Workforce Rises from April to May
item.description: The number of employees in the oil and gas extraction industry increased slightly from April to May, data on the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics website showed.

item.title: DOE Plans $17.5B Loans for Westinghouse-Backed Nuclear Projects
item.description: The United States Department of Energy announced a conditional loan commitment of $17.5 billion to help 5 nuclear power projects procure long-lead items to deploy Westinghouse's advanced reactor.

item.title: Var Energi Greenlights Gjoa Projects
item.description: Var Energi and its partners made a final investment decision to proceed with the Gjoa Subsea Projects on Norway's side of the North Sea, targeting about 76 million barrels of oil equivalent gross proven and probable…

item.title: China Isn’t Giving Up On Coal Despite Green Energy Goals
item.description: Chinese authorities are not restricting coal power growth in the new five-year energy plan as Beijing doubles down on the fossil fuel as a “bottom-line guarantee” of the electricity system amid rising shares of…

item.title: Why Is Oil Trading Down Today?
item.description: Why is the oil price going lower on Friday?

item.title: Equinor submits Wisting oilfield EIA for public review
item.description: Equinor and its partners have submitted a proposed environmental impact assessment (EIA) programme for the potential development of the Wisting oil field, which is now open for public consultation.

item.title: Baghdad Hosting EU Energy Talks After Iraq Floats OPEC Exit
item.description: European officials are expected to visit Baghdad in the coming weeks for high-level talks on energy cooperation, just hours after Iraq warned it could leave OPEC unless the producer group grants the country a higher…

item.title: Baker Hughes wins contract from Azule Energy for Greater PAJ project
item.description: Baker Hughes has been selected by Azule Energy to provide subsea production systems for the Greater PAJ project, an offshore oil development in Angola’s Blocks 31 and 31/21.

item.title: Naftogaz, Orlen Ink New LNG, Decarbonization Agreements
item.description: 'The first agreement provides for assessing opportunities to increase gas supply volumes and jointly use regasification terminals and gas transmission infrastructure across the Baltic region, Central and Eastern Europe'.

item.title: What Happened to Big Oil's Green Pivot?
item.description: Over the past 20 years, the story seemed straightforward. Big Oil would gradually become Big Energy. Over time, oil majors would use their balance sheets, engineering expertise, and global project-management skills to…

item.title: Strike Averted as Norway Onshore Support Workers Agree New Terms
item.description: The Offshore Norge oil and gas association and the union Styrke agreed pay increases and other changes in the Supply Base Agreement, averting a planned strike by about 875 workers.

item.title: Output at Key Field in Kazakhstan Takes Hit from Attack on Russian Plant
item.description: Kazakhstan's giant Karachaganak oil and gas field cut crude production by more than a quarter after a drone attack forced the shutdown of a processing plant in Russia that handles its gas.

item.title: Saudi Arabia Set to Slash Oil Prices as Hormuz Reopens
item.description: Saudi Arabia is expected to slash the official selling prices of its crude loading for Asia in August, as Middle East’s crude benchmarks crashed amid the tentative reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the oil supply…

item.title: ONGC signs technical services deal with bp for Western Offshore Basin
item.description: Oil and Natural Gas Corporation Limited (ONGC), an Indian central public sector undertaking, has awarded a technical services contract to BP to help boost production from its fields in the Western Offshore Basin.

item.title: Kazakhstan Cuts Gas Output after Drone Strike on Russian Processing Plant
item.description: Kazakhstan has slashed by a quarter natural gas production at the huge Karachaganak oil and gas field after Ukraine earlier this week hit with drones a Russian plant processing part of the gas, Kazakhstan’s Energy…

item.title: Crude Prices Sink as Traders Bet on More Iranian Oil
item.description: August WTI crude oil futures kept falling sharply during the week ending June 26. Traders continued to take out the extra price they had added earlier because they worried about possible supply problems in the Middle…

item.title: China’s Crude Imports Set to Hit Weakest Level Since 2016
item.description: Chinese crude oil imports this month are on course to book an even weaker month than May, according to Kpler data, which sees the daily average at just 6.4 million barrels, as cited by Bloomberg. Data from Vortexa…

item.title: Hormuz Shipping Confidence Is Still Shaky
item.description: Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz has picked up over the past week but shipowners remain cautious about transiting, especially after the Thursday attack in the Gulf of Oman on a vessel that had just cleared the…
